summary: Excellent soundboard transfer of a killer ’73 show highlighted by a stellar Here Comes Sunshine and a thunderous Other One.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑mastered soundboard capture (aside from a missing Phil solo patch) that lets the band’s energy shine through. Fans rave about the first‑set strength – especially “Here Comes Sunshine” and “Playin’ in the Band” – and the crushing second‑set “Other One”. Overall the performance is praised as one of the best of 1973.

ai_rating.rationale: Multiple enthusiastic reviews praise both the musical performance and the high‑quality transfer; only a minor omission (Phil solo) prevents a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean transfer praised by listeners; one note about missing Phil Lesh solo patch but no major audio flaws reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as one of the stronger ’73 sets, highlighted by HCS and PITB; second‑set powerfully driven by a monumental Other One.

band_member_comments.Phil: Solo before The Other One is missing (not patched in).
